explicit_text: I was born in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, India in 1998. Although my family moved to California before I was born, I came into this world in Ujjain because my parents wanted me to be born in India. It holds a special place in my story as the starting point of my life before I moved back and forth between India and the US.

explicit_text: Madhya Pradesh is the Indian state where I was born, specifically in the city of Ujjain. It was in Madhya Pradesh that I settled into middle and high school, embracing my Indian identity more deeply.

explicit_text: India is where I was born and where I spent important parts of my childhood and adolescence. My family moved back to India after living in California, and I adapted to life there, attending school and embracing Indian culture. Even though I have an American passport, India shaped much of who I am, from language to traditions like Diwali and Bollywood music.

explicit_text: California was my home during my early childhood until fifth grade, as my father moved there in 1994 to work for Microsoft. California represents the American part of my upbringing before we moved back to India.

explicit_text: I came to the University of Minnesota to study after finishing high school in India. It was at the U of M campus that I began to deeply question my identity as Indian American. Being there made me reflect on what it means to belong to two cultures and how I fit into both worlds.
